mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the equity risk premium and discount rates, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets compress wage growth and trigger Fed tightening expectations, which compress equity multiples and drive negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation persists above 320.0, the Federal Reserve is forced to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of periods where inflation exceeds the 320.0 threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 index levels and generates positive forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate positively with subsequent 20-day SPX returns, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above 105.0, supply chain bottlenecks clear and capacity utilization expands, boosting profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO crosses the 105.0 threshold, or the signal produces false breakouts in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and rising unemployment signals a structural labor market mismatch that eventually stalls wage-driven consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict negative equity returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, surging consumer credit fuels asset price inflation and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit positive skew when consumer credit expands under the zero lower bound, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy falls below 55%.
